summary: Matched the map-collision function `mpColl_800477E0` in `src/melee/mp/mpcoll.c`, resolving #1064. The implementation preserves/restores ECB bottom coordinates when `x130_flags & 0x10` is set, updates collision state through existing `mpColl_*` helpers, computes a static boolean `mpColl_804D649C` from ECB correction extents against `flt_804D7FD8`, calls `mpColl_80043754(mpColl_80046904, arg0, 1U)`, forwards the result into `mpColl_80043324`, and returns it as `bool`. The PR also reorganized `mpcoll.h` into an address-commented prototype list, moved the old broader header contents into a new `mpcoll.dox`, and updated object-file bookkeeping.
what_changed: Added a C implementation of `bool mpColl_800477E0(CollData* arg0)` to `src/melee/mp/mpcoll.c`.
Added file-scope `static bool mpColl_804D649C;` used by the matched function.

`mpColl_800477E0` now calls `mpColl_80041C8C`, conditionally preserves `arg0->x84_ecb.bottom.{x,y}`, dispatches either `mpColl_800424DC(arg0, 6U)` or `mpColl_8004293C(arg0)` depending on `arg0->x104`, calls `mpColl_80042384`, snapshots `env_flags` into `x138`, clears `env_flags`, computes `mpColl_804D649C`, calls `mpColl_80043754`, then calls `mpColl_80043324` with the returned value.
Changed the `mpColl_800477E0` declaration from `void mpColl_800477E0(CollData*)` to `bool mpColl_800477E0(CollData*)` in the public header.
Reworked `src/melee/mp/mpcoll.h` to use the guard `GALE01_041C78`, remove the direct `ft/fighter.h` include, and present prototypes with address comments such as `/* 0477E0 */`.
Created `src/melee/mp/mpcoll.dox` containing the previous broad prototype/header content, including the `MELEE_MP_MPCOLL_H` guard and Doxygen-style `Coll_InitECB` note.

Updated `asm/melee/mp/mpcoll.s`; the old raw `.global mpColl_800477E0` assembly block was replaced by generated-style `.fn mpColl_800477E0, global` / `.endfn mpColl_800477E0` output, and the file gained `.file "mpcoll.c"`.
smart_moves: The match preserves `x84_ecb.bottom.x` and `.y` only when the original flag condition requires it, mirroring the control-flow dependency visible in the target behavior rather than simplifying it away.
The return from `mpColl_80043754` is explicitly stored as `bool ret` and returned as a boolean; the author left an evidence note that the callback return is only set to 0 or 1.

The implementation reuses existing `mpColl_*` helper functions instead of open-coding surrounding collision update logic, which keeps the matched function small and aligned with known call boundaries.
The header cleanup adds address comments to prototypes, making future cross-referencing and symbol search easier in a partially decompiled module.
Moving the older header contents to `mpcoll.dox` preserves useful declaration/documentation material while allowing the compiled header to be tightened.
decomp_lessons: When a callee/callback chain only produces 0 or 1, using `bool` for the matched function return can be important for matching and for documenting intent.

Preserve temporary field values around helper calls when flags indicate the original code protects those fields; here, ECB bottom coordinates are saved and restored around ECB/collision refresh helpers.
For large modules, address-commented prototypes in headers are useful searchable anchors and help keep function-order knowledge close to declarations.

Header dependencies matter for matching and build stability: removing an unnecessary direct include such as `ft/fighter.h` from a normal header can reduce coupling, while `.dox` can retain documentation-only declarations.

Static file-scope state may need to be introduced with the exact storage class and type used by a matched routine; this PR introduces `static bool mpColl_804D649C` alongside the function that writes it.

Diffs for a single matched function in a large asm file may be huge because generated assembly formatting and object boundaries change; inspect the C diff and symbol-specific hunk before inferring broad logic changes.
naming_conventions: Unidentified map-collision functions keep the `mpColl_800xxxxx` address-based naming style.
Header prototypes are prefixed with address comments like `/* 0477E0 */ bool mpColl_800477E0(CollData*);`.
Collision data parameters commonly use `CollData*` and, where names are known, short names such as `cd`; unknown parameters remain `arg0`, `arg1`, etc.
ECB-related local names use descriptive prefixes such as `ecb_x` and `ecb_y` when derived from `x84_ecb.bottom`.
Documentation-only legacy declarations were moved to `mpcoll.dox` while the compiled header uses the address-derived guard `GALE01_041C78`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Matched `mpColl_800477E0` by adding equivalent C and replacing the old raw assembly block for that symbol in `asm/melee/mp/mpcoll.s` with generated `.fn`/`.endfn` assembly output.
Used explicit casts and constants visible in the C implementation, including `(s32) arg0->x104 == 1`, `6U`, and `1U`, to preserve codegen-sensitive behavior.
Retained a scratch/reference comment for the callback return path: `mpColl_80043754` returns `mpColl_80046904`'s return value, described as `var_22` from a decomp.me scratch and likely boolean.
Computed `mpColl_804D649C` using two ECB correction height comparisons against `flt_804D7FD8`; this avoids introducing unexplained magic literals in the matched C.
Kept helper-call ordering explicit: reset/update collision state, preserve selected ECB bottom values, set `x138`/clear `env_flags`, then call the callback walker and final `mpColl_80043324` update.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body only states `Resolves #1064.`, so intent beyond matching `mpColl_800477E0` is inferred from the diff rather than reviewer discussion.
follow_up_queries: Inspect issue #1064 for the original scratch/matching discussion around `mpColl_800477E0`.
Search later PRs for `mpColl_804D649C` to see whether the static boolean was renamed or its semantics were discovered.
Search later PRs touching `mpcoll.h` or `mpcoll.dox` for conventions around moving declarations from compiled headers into documentation-only `.dox` files.
Look for later matches of neighboring functions `mpColl_800471F8`, `mpColl_800473CC`, `mpColl_800478F4`, and `mpColl_80047A08` to reuse the ECB preservation and callback-return patterns.
Check whether `mpColl_80046904` was later matched/renamed to confirm the boolean return assumption noted in this PR.
